title: buildah treadmill: documentation and process updates
body:
Many small improvements (reviewable as individual commits).

Fixes: RUN-2217

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This is what the task map looks like in the treadmill (see orange-yellow block in middle) ![cirrus-map-vendor_buildah_latest](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/91d559b0-0179-4372-9c87-71d306a82de5)

I actually agree, and spent a long time thinking about it. Decided to skip CI because:
1. treadmill script is not run anywhere in CI
2. run-buildah-bud-tests only changed wording inside quotes, and I tested (locally) that there are no errors introduced.

Given that, would you still like me to repush and run CI?
If you also made sure the pre-commit hook with the spell checker and trailing whitespace/newline check, etc... pass then I can accept it, changes LGTM.
